perturbation.original_text: A. Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber will cease any further funding and/or other support for the BRI Project. Further Bioamber will require that any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project indefinitely, irrespective of whether the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Bioamber will continue funding and/or other support for the BRI Project at its sole discretion. Further Bioamber may, at its discretion, utilize any unexpended funds received from Bioamber for the BRI Project or any other project it deems suitable."
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates a clear condition for the termination of Bioamber's funding for the BRI Project (achievement of a specific production level). The modified text removes this condition, allowing Bioamber to fund the project indefinitely and use unexpended funds at its discretion, directly contradicting the original clause. This change introduces uncertainty about the financial obligations of Bioamber regarding the BRI project, creating a legal ambiguity. The contradiction occurs in section 13.9 with respect to termination of funding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project indefinitely, irrespective of whether the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Bioamber will continue funding and/or other support for the BRI Project at its sole discretion. Further Bioamber may, at its discretion, utilize any unexpended funds received from Bioamber for the BRI Project or any other project it deems suitable."
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a condition (achievement of succinic acid production levels) for the termination of Bioamber's funding. The changed text removes this condition, stating Bioamber can fund the project indefinitely and use unexpended funds at its discretion. This directly contradicts the original clause's defined termination event, introducing uncertainty about the financial obligations and creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding section 13.9.

perturbation.original_text: B. New Section 13.10 is added to the Development Agreement as set forth below: "13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to Bioamber hereby agreeing to convert the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to solely utilize CB1 as the biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: B. New Section 13.10 is added to the Development Agreement as set forth below: "13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, and Bioamber shall have the option, but not the obligation, to convert the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to utilize CB1 or any other biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof).
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Bioamber to convert its facilities to solely utilize CB1. The modified text grants Bioamber the option to convert or not, contradicting the mandatory conversion requirement. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about Bioamber's obligation regarding the biocatalyst conversion. The contradiction stems in section 13.10 in the obligation to use CB1 Strain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. New Section 13.10 is added to the Development Agreement as set forth below: "13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, and Bioamber shall have the option, but not the obligation, to convert the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to utilize CB1 or any other biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof).
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the conversion to CB1, while the changed text provides an option, not an obligation, to convert to CB1 or any other biocatalyst. This directly contradicts the previous requirement, creating uncertainty about Bioamber's responsibilities and aligning with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' and 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definitions, as the obligation has been removed, causing a conflict with the initial mandatory statement found within 13.10.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in these amendments will increase Bioamber's obligations related to CB1, MCC-17 or E. coli strain. Bioamber has the sole discretion to decide whether or not to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, anything in Section 5.9 of the Commercial License to the contrary notwithstanding.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that Bioamber's obligation to replace strains remains unchanged. The modified text reverses this, stating that Bioamber has the sole discretion to decide whether or not to replace strains, essentially negating the original obligation. This in-text contradiction makes the obligations about the strains used, uncertain, potentially leading to breach of contract claims. The contradiction happens due to the modified text in Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in these amendments will increase Bioamber's obligations related to CB1, MCC-17 or E. coli strain. Bioamber has the sole discretion to decide whether or not to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, anything in Section 5.9 of the Commercial License to the contrary notwithstanding.
perturbation.justification: The original text affirmed Bioamber's obligation to replace strains. The changed text grants Bioamber sole discretion, effectively negating that obligation. This creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text and the Changed Text are in the same location. However, the Change negates the original obligation creating a contradiction, which makes the enforcement of either obligation about strain replacement uncertain and unenforceable, meeting the definition of a structural flaw-induced in-text contradiction.
